pr.title: Remove reference to Trello in docs
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Removed an obsolete Trello-board reference from the getting-started documentation. The deleted paragraph had told new contributors to check a public Trello board for claimed assembly files and to join Discord for write access. The surrounding guidance about searching the repo for an undecompiled label such as `ftColl_8007B8CC` remained intact.
what_changed: Deleted a two-line paragraph from `docs/getting_started.md` that linked to the public Trello board `https://trello.com/b/pz2ACtnS/melee-decompilation`.
Removed guidance that contributors should avoid `.s` files claimed on Trello.
Removed the note that the Trello link was read-only and that Discord access was needed for write access.
Kept the next onboarding step: search the repository for a function label, for example `ftColl_8007B8CC`, to verify it has not already been decompiled.
smart_moves: Simplified contributor onboarding by removing a stale external project-management dependency from the docs.
Avoided changing unrelated decompilation guidance; the PR only removed the Trello-specific paragraph.
Reduced risk of misleading new contributors about claim-tracking workflow if Trello was no longer authoritative.
decomp_lessons: Keep onboarding docs aligned with the project’s current coordination process; stale claim-tracking links can misdirect new decomp contributors.
When removing obsolete process guidance, preserve still-useful matching/decomp workflow instructions such as searching for existing C implementations by symbol label.
Small documentation-only PRs can be valuable when they remove outdated external dependencies from contributor workflow.
naming_conventions: The retained documentation example uses a decomp-style function label `ftColl_8007B8CC`, combining subsystem-like prefix/camel segment with an address suffix.
No new naming convention was introduced in this PR.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The retained guidance recommends choosing assembly that is not already decompiled and checking by searching the repo for the symbol label.
The docs continue to suggest using `grep -rn 'ftColl_8007B8CC' src` as a quick check for existing implementations.
No assembly matching code or tactics changed directly; this was a documentation cleanup.
searchable_terms: Remove reference to Trello in docs
follow_up_queries: Where is the current authoritative place for contributors to coordinate or claim decompilation work after Trello removal?
Are there any remaining references to Trello or outdated claim-tracking workflows in other documentation files?
Should `docs/getting_started.md` mention the current Discord/GitHub-based coordination process if one exists?
